codes.name: Alien Labor Certification
codes.description: Under the H-2A program, local AJCC staff developed a hiring agreement with an employer that took into account the local AJCC Site's resources, but that did not adversely affect the site's ability to adequately serve other employers. The hiring agreement may not include provisions for AJCC staff to assist the employer in pre-employment activities such as verifying prior employment or obtaining references.

codes.name: Americans with Disabilities
codes.description: AJCC contacted an employer that submitted a job posting that appeared to be discriminatory under the American with Disabilities Act of 1990. In contacting the employer, AJCC provided legal job posting requirements, and informed the employer that unless the job posting's specifications can be legally justified, that the employer's job posting request could not be accommodated.

codes.name: CalJOBS Assistance
codes.description: AJCC staff provided an employer with information regarding CalJOBS, or assisted an employer with registering in CalJOBS.

codes.name: Apprenticeship
codes.description: AJCC staff provided an employer with information regarding apprenticeship programs, or assisted an employer with registering in CalJOBS.

codes.name: Bonding
codes.description: The Designated AJCC staff person responsible for providing and/or arranging for bonding services, completed the required steps to provide an employer with a fidelity bond for an at-risk or hard-to-place participant. These steps include, but are not limited to, ensuring the employer meets all eligibility requirements, completing the EDD Fidelity Bonding Certification Request, completing the EDD Fidelity Bonding Employer Confirmation Letter, and submitting bonding requests to the Workforce Services' Division Bonding Coordinator.

codes.name: CalJOBS External Recruitment
codes.description: After determining that the CalJOBS job-seeker participant pool did not include qualified individuals for an employer's specific job opening, AJCC staff conducted recruitment efforts to locate qualified individuals. These efforts include, but are not limited to, contacting colleges, trade schools, professional organizations, and other employment and training providers who may have access to individuals with the employer's required skills and qualifications.

codes.name: Disability Insurance
codes.description: An AJCC sponsored a Disability Insurance seminar for employers.

codes.name: Employer Advisory Council (EAC)
codes.description: AJCC staff provided an employer information regarding the Employer Advisory Council, or provided AJCC Services to an employer at an Employer Advisory Council event.

codes.name: Employer Application Issuance
codes.description: At the request of an employer, AJCC staff issued the employer's work application to job seekers. Prior to agreeing to issue the employer's work application, the staff member reviewed the work application to ensure compliance with the Department of Fair Employment and Housing pre-employment guidelines.

codes.name: Employer Recall
codes.description: AJCC staff suspended an employer's access to CalJOBS after determining the employer violated Workforce Services' regulations and/or Federal, state, or local employment-related laws. Reasons for access suspension include, but are not limited to: the employer includes discriminatory specifications in the job listing; fails to adhere to Federal, State, or local employment-related laws; misrepresents terms or conditions of employment; causes Workforce Services to receive substantiated formal or informal complaints from job seekers; and posts an invalid job opening, such as one due to a trade dispute.

codes.name: Employment Training Panel (ETP)
codes.description: AJCC staff assisted an employer with information regarding the Employment Training Panel's (ETP) available programs and services, including eligibility requirements, application assistance and programs specific to an employer's business. This definition does not include Training for New Employees (E40), or Training for Current Employees (E41).

codes.name: Employment Law
codes.description: An AJCC sponsored a labor law seminar for employers.

codes.name: Employment Taxes
codes.description: An AJCC sponsored a payroll tax seminar for employers.

codes.name: Enterprise Zone (EZ) Tax Credits
codes.description: AJCC staff identified a potentially eligible job seeker and referred him/her to the nearest Enterprise Zone-certifying agency or Enterprise Zone employer. Staff may not perform eligibility determinations, request documentation, or sign the Enterprise Tax Voucher (DOC EZ1).

codes.name: Exclusive Hiring Agreement
codes.description: AJCC staff established an agreement with an employer that requires all job seekers to apply through CalJOBS.

codes.name: Federal Contractor Job Listing
codes.description: AJCC staff assisted an employer with federal job contractor job listing. Staff ensured that the federal contractor was properly identified, and that its special employment needs, particularly those relating to qualified veterans' hiring, were met to the fullest possible extent.

codes.name: Fraud Reporting
codes.description: After receiving a job listing from a questionable employer, AJCC staff ensured the request was genuine, and represented an actual job opportunity. Questionable employer examples include employers who require unusual pre-employment action on the job-seeker's part (e.g. a deposit for some alleged service, such as training, transportation dues, fees, or food and lodging; employers who require monetary deposits or payments), unknown employers who request permission to interview at the AJCC; employers who ask job seekers to be sent to an address that's not a normal place of business (e.g., hotel room), unknown employer who offer to provide transportation to the job site, and employers whose address or job location is vague.

codes.name: High Density Unemployment Area Resident
codes.description: AJCC staff assisted an employer in hiring a high-density unemployment area resident.

codes.name: Internship
codes.description: AJCC staff entered an internship into CalJOBS for an employer, or publicized the internship at the AJCC.

codes.name: Job Development Contact
codes.description: AJCC Staff contacted an employer regarding job development contact participation. If the employer is not registered, then staff would need to complete the employer's CalJOBS' registration to collect the employer's information.

codes.name: Job Fairs
codes.description: AJCC staff partnered with an appropriate entity within the community to present and/or participate in a job fair.

codes.name: Job Identification Contact
codes.description: AJCC staff contacted a CalJOBS registered employer for available postings.

codes.name: Job Listing Assistance
codes.description: AJCC staff assisted a CalJOBS-registered employer with filling out a job listing form (DE 8203), or AJCC staff completed a job order listing in CalJOBS on behalf of the employer. This service does not include CalJOBS Assistance (E03).

codes.name: Job Referral
codes.description: BSR referred employers to participants and informed them of open positions within the company.

codes.name: Job Shadowing
codes.description: AJCC staff coordinated a job shadowing experience for a qualified participant with an employer. The employer would like to hire a participant, but would first like the participant to do a job shadowing.

codes.name: Labor Market Information
codes.description: AJCC staff provided an employer with national, state, or local area's market information to assist in the employer's business plan expansion, relocation, or future hiring and training needs; or, referred the employer to a labor market information consultant who prepared detailed occupational labor market information for the employer.

codes.name: Local Planning, Zoning, Permits
codes.description: AJCC staff provided an employer with Local Planning, Zoning, and Permits information.

codes.name: Marketing Package
codes.description: AJCC staff provided employer marketing materials containing information regarding EDD services. Materials could include, but are not limited to, DE forms, brochures geared towards the employer, Labor Market Information Fact Sheet, and flyers.

codes.name: New Employee Registry
codes.description: AJCC staff provided an employer with specific information pertaining to the California New Employee Registry.

codes.name: On-The-Job Training
codes.description: AJCC staff established an on-the-job training contract with an employer. Under the contract, the employer provided occupational training to a WIOA participant in exchange for a specific percentage reimbursement of the participant's wage rate, and received technical assistance from AJCC staff throughout the life of the training contract.

codes.name: Older Worker Hiring
codes.description: AJCC staff conducted outreach efforts to employers highlighting the potential of older workers and their contributions to a diverse workforce. The outreach efforts highlighted traits that make older workers ideal candidates for employment, including their reliability, commitment, enthusiasm, and a keen know-how that comes with a lifetime of experience.

codes.name: Rapid Response (layoff or closure)
codes.description: AJCC staff provided rapid response activities in the case of a permanent closure or mass layoff at a plant, facility or enterprise, or a natural or other disaster that resulted in mass job dislocation in order to assist dislocated workers in obtaining reemployment as soon as possible. Rapid response activities include, but are not limited to, establishing on-site contact with employers and employee representatives; providing information and access to available employment and training activities; providing an on-site presentation to employees on when to file for, how to file for, and who qualifies for unemployment insurance; assisting in establishing a labor-management committee; voluntarily agreed to by labor and management, with the ability to devise and implement a strategy for assessing the employment and training needs of Dislocated Workers, and obtaining services to meet such needs; providing emergency assistance adapted to the particular closure, layoffs or disaster; and providing assistance to the local community in developing a coordinated response and in obtaining access to state economic development assistance.

codes.name: Recruitment at EDD
codes.description: An employer used an AJCC's facility and scheduling services to screen and interview job-seeking participants.

codes.name: Reverse Referral Agreement
codes.description: AJCC staff established an agreement with an employer that it would provide a handout or poster to encourage job seekers to enroll and enter a resume in CalJOBS. The rationale for this agreement is that when an employer has openings and lists them in the CalJOBS those job seekers' resumes can be accessed by the employer. This Agreement reduces business interruptions to the employer when there are no openings, and expands the job-seeker participant pool in CalJOBS.

codes.name: Resume Referral
codes.description: AJCC staff conducted a resume search to find job-seeking participant resumes that match an employer's job requirements, and provided those resumes to the employer.

codes.name: Career Pathways Programs
codes.description: AJCC staff coordinated a focused recruitment campaign for a new business venture, or a facility needing a large number of specialized workers.

codes.name: Targeted Recruitment
codes.description: AJCC staff coordinated a focused recruitment campaign for a new business venture, or a facility needing a large number of specialized workers.

codes.name: Tax Credit Programs
codes.description: AJCC staff informed an employer about available hiring incentives and tax credits including, but not limited to, the Work Opportunity Tax Credit (WOTC), empowerment zone/renewal community tax incentives, the disabled access credit, and the architectural and transportation tax deduction. This definition does not include the Veteran Hiring Incentive Program (E43).

codes.name: Trade Credit Programs
codes.description: AJCC staff informed an employer of Trade Assistance Act (TAA) associated programs.

codes.name: Training for New Employees
codes.description: AJCC staff informed an employer of funding available through the Employment Training Panel (ETP) for workforce training programs, and referred it to the ETP website or phone number for additional information.

codes.name: Training for Current Employees
codes.description: AJCC staff informed an employer of funding available through the Employment Training Panel (ETP) for workforce training programs, and referred it to the ETP website or phone number for additional information.

codes.name: Unemployment Insurance
codes.description: AJCC staff sponsored a seminar for employers regarding Unemployment Insurance.

codes.name: Veteran Hiring Incentive Program
codes.description: AJCC staff informed an employer about the Work Opportunity Tax Credit (WOTC) and the 'qualified Veterans' is one of the WOTC target groups that may qualify the employer for a tax credit.

codes.name: Welfare Recipient Hiring
codes.description: AJCC staff assisted an employer in hiring a welfare recipient.

codes.name: Work Experience Programs
codes.description: AJCC staff coordinated with an employer to provide a participant with a Work Experience Opportunity, and technical assistance throughout the life of the contract. Work Experience is a structured learning experience that takes place in a workplace for a limited time period. It may be paid or unpaid, as appropriate, and may be in private for-profit sector, or the public sector.

codes.name: Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act (WIOA)
codes.description: AJCC staff informed an employer of available Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act employer services that include, but are not limited to, customized training, on-the-job-training reimbursement assistance, and training subsidies.

codes.name: Youth Employment Opportunities
codes.description: A Youth Employment Opportunity Program (YEOP) Specialist contacted an employer for the purposes of informing the employer about YEOP, and encouraging the employer to hire a Youth participant. To be used after site has been approved.

codes.name: Follow-up with Employer on New Hires
codes.description: AJCC staff contacted the employer to determine if a hired participant was successful, and to provide as-needed additional assistance to the employer to ensure satisfaction with the business services provided by AJCC staff.

codes.name: Follow-up with Employer on Referred Candidate
codes.description: AJCC staff contacted an employer via phone, email, or in person to follow-up on a candidate referred by staff to a CalJOBS job listing.

codes.name: Employer Networking
codes.description: AJCC staff provided an employer with information regarding AJCC services in an effort to establish a relationship, or to maintain an existing relationship.

codes.name: Referral to Community Resource
codes.description: AJCC staff provided a proactive linkage and referral to community resources that support an employer's workforce needs.

codes.name: Small Business Downsizing/Closure Assistance
codes.description: AJCC staff provided services to a small business. Assistance may include onsite visits to provide the employer with information on the available AJCC services for affected employees and referrals to appropriate community resources.

codes.name: Engage in Strategic Planning/Economic Development
codes.description: AJCC staff engaged in workforce investment strategic planning or business growth and economic development strategic planning. These activities include, but are not limited to, participating in community-based strategic planning; sponsoring employer forums; securing information on industry trends; providing information for the purpose of corporate economic development planning; partnering in collaborative efforts to identify workforce challenges; and developing ways to address those challenges.

codes.name: Follow-up with Employer on Employer Services
codes.description: AJCC staff contacted the employer to determine if employer services that were provided by the AJCC were successful. To be done the following day.

codes.name: Youth Outreach and Marketing Presentation
codes.description: Staff conducted presentations at entities (e.g., employers, community-based organizations, schools, workforce partners) in the community serving youth on the available EDD and partner youth services and resources as part of the YEOP expansion effort.

codes.name: Youth Outreach and Marketing Contact
codes.description: Staff contacted entities (e.g., employers, community-based organizations, schools, workforce partners) serving youth to schedule an appointment to provide information on the available EDD and partner youth services and resources as part of the YEOP.

codes.name: Notification to Employer of Potential Applicant
codes.description: Staff notified the client of potential job. This code is system generated at time of client notification.

codes.name: Supportive Service: Transportation Assistance TAP Card
codes.description: Participant is requesting assistance for a TAP CARD to commute to and from AJCC, Job search and vocational training search. Participant does not have an automobile and requires transportation assistance.

codes.name: Incentives/Bonuses
codes.description: Participant is requesting supportive services in the amount of $300 incentive for completion of all 6 INVEST workshops. Completion of 5 modules on the GetYourEdge.org. Completed: Fair chance workshops with INVEST BTS, budgets & finance basics, social media & online presence, developing a professional image, active listening, and dealing with difficult situations.

codes.name: Clothing Voucher
codes.description: Participant is requesting assistance for clothing. Participant is scheduled to start school or work and does not have sufficient amount of clothing due to financial insecurity.
